bb90b1f8ccd06830d730f34521a797f6c3aa2411.title: Unique PPML Projection
bb90b1f8ccd06830d730f34521a797f6c3aa2411.body:
Fix a number of periods \(T\), a finite cohort set \(\mathcal C\), cohort shares \(\pi_g\in(0,1)\), positive limiting baseline means \(\bar b_g>0\), time components \(\gamma_{t0}\), and proportional-effect log multipliers \(\delta_{gt}\). Suppose the collapsed design rank condition in Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:collapsed-design-rank} holds for \((T,\mathcal C,\pi)\). Let \(\theta^\star(\delta)\), \(L(\theta;\delta)\), \(m_{gt}(\delta)\), and \(\mu^\star_{gt}(\delta)\) be as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:collapsed-population-projection}. Then \(\theta^\star(\delta)\) is the unique global maximizer of \(L(\theta;\delta)\). Moreover, for every collapsed nuisance regressor index \(j\),
\[
\sum_{g\in\mathcal C}\sum_{t=1}^T
q_{gt} X_{gt,j}\{m_{gt}(\delta)-\mu^\star_{gt}(\delta)\}=0,
\]
and the treatment score also vanishes:
\[
\sum_{g\in\mathcal C}\sum_{t=1}^T
q_{gt} D_{gt}\{m_{gt}(\delta)-\mu^\star_{gt}(\delta)\}=0.
\]

71e5d6c15315edcbef1a3dabc17824aa8d342f28.title: Unit FE Collapse
71e5d6c15315edcbef1a3dabc17824aa8d342f28.body:
Let $T$ be a number of periods and let $\mathcal C$ be a finite cohort set. Suppose:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Horizon.)} $T\ge 4$.
\item \textbf{(Cohort support.)} The never-treated cohort belongs to $\mathcal C$, and every finite cohort in $\mathcal C$ is dated after the first period.
\item \textbf{(Array support.)} For every $N$ and every unit $i$, the cohort label $G_i$ belongs to $\mathcal C$.
\item \textbf{(Positive primitives.)} The unit baselines $b_{iN}$ and the limiting within-cohort baselines $\bar b_g$ are strictly positive, and each limiting cohort share satisfies $\pi_g\in(0,1)$.
\item \textbf{(Share limits.)} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:cohort-share-limit} holds for $G_i$ and $\pi_g$.
\item \textbf{(Untreated means.)} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:unit-untreated-exponential-mean} holds for $Y_{it}(d)$, $\mathcal P_N$, $b_{iN}$, and $\gamma_{t0}$.
\item \textbf{(Baseline limits.)} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:within-cohort-baseline-limit} holds for $G_i$, $b_{iN}$, and $\bar b_g$.
\item \textbf{(Proportional effects.)} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:proportional-effects} holds for $Y_{it}(d)$, $\mathcal P_N$, $G_i$, and $\delta_{gt}$.
\item \textbf{(Collapsed rank.)} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:collapsed-design-rank} holds for $\mathcal C$ and $\pi_g$.
\end{itemize}
Then, for every $N$ with $|\mathcal C|\le N$, the finite-array unit-FE population criterion $L_N(\theta_N;\delta)$ of Definition~\ref{obj:def:unit-fe-population-projection} has a unique global maximizer, the collapsed finite-array criterion $L_{Nc}(\theta;\delta)$ has a unique global maximizer, and
\[
\beta_N^\star(\delta)
=
\text{the treatment coordinate of the unique maximizer of }L_{Nc}(\theta;\delta).
\]
Moreover,
\[
\beta_N^\star(\delta)\longrightarrow \beta^\star(\delta)
\qquad\text{as }N\to\infty .
\]
Finally, for every $N$ with $|\mathcal C|\le N$ and every alternative positive baseline array $b'_{iN}$, if
\[
\bar b'_{gN}=\bar b_{gN}
\qquad\text{for every }g\in\mathcal C,
\]
then replacing $b_{iN}$ by $b'_{iN}$ leaves the finite-array unit-FE coefficient unchanged:
\[
\beta_N^\star(\delta;b')=\beta_N^\star(\delta;b).
\]

ea87dfa5cd2924bb22982cac38e52bdc7afc13f5.title: Four-Cohort Sign Reversal
ea87dfa5cd2924bb22982cac38e52bdc7afc13f5.body:
The four-cohort witness \(W_4\) is constructed with cohort support
\(\mathcal C=\{2,3,4,\infty\}\), equal shares \(\pi_g=1/4\), limiting baselines
\(\bar b_g=1\), untreated time component \(\gamma_{t0}=0\), and proportional-effect
log multipliers
\[
\delta_{gt}=
\begin{cases}
\log 4, & (g,t)=(2,4),\\
\log(101/100), & D_{gt}=1 \text{ and } (g,t)\neq(2,4),\\
0, & D_{gt}=0 .
\end{cases}
\]
Then:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Region membership.)} The primitive \(W_4\) belongs to the sign-reversal region \(\mathcal R_4\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:global-sign-reversal-region}.
\item \textbf{(Positive effects.)} Every treated supported cell has a strictly positive proportional-effect log multiplier: if \(g\in\mathcal C\), \(t\in\{1,2,3,4\}\), and \(D_{gt}=1\), then \(\delta_{gt}>0\).
\item \textbf{(Unique largest treated effect.)} The treated cell \((2,4)\) has the strictly largest proportional-effect log multiplier: if \(g\in\mathcal C\), \(t\in\{1,2,3,4\}\), \(D_{gt}=1\), and \((g,t)\neq(2,4)\), then \(\delta_{gt}<\delta_{2,4}\).
\item \textbf{(Weighted FWL residuals.)} At the no-effect vector, the weighted FWL residual \(\widetilde W_{gt}\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:weighted-fwl-residual} equals
\[
\frac{1}{8}
\begin{pmatrix}
-3 & 3 & 1 & -1\\
-1 & -3 & 3 & 1\\
1 & -1 & -3 & 3\\
3 & 1 & -1 & -3
\end{pmatrix},
\]
with rows \(g=2,3,4,\infty\) and columns \(t=1,2,3,4\). In particular, \(\widetilde W_{2,4}=-1/8\).
\item \textbf{(Negative late-cell derivative at zero.)} Holding all other effects at zero,
\[
\left.\frac{d}{dx}\,
\beta^\star\!\left(\delta_{2,4}=x,\ \delta_{gt}=0\text{ for }(g,t)\neq(2,4)\right)
\right|_{x=0}
=-\frac{1}{10}.
\]
\item \textbf{(Local negative derivative under positive effects.)} There exists \(\varepsilon>0\) such that, for every effect vector \(\delta'\) satisfying \(|\delta'_{gt}|<\varepsilon\) for all \(g\in\mathcal C\) and \(t\in\{1,2,3,4\}\), if \(\delta'\) has strictly positive treated effects in the sense of Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:strict-positive-effects}, then
\[
\left.
\frac{d}{dx}\,
\beta^\star\!\left(\delta' \text{ with } \delta'_{2,4}\text{ replaced by }x\right)
\right|_{x=\delta'_{2,4}}
<0 .
\]
\end{itemize}

aa95f015933219c08874f10058ddaf0b40192a9e.title: Sharp PPML Sign
aa95f015933219c08874f10058ddaf0b40192a9e.body:
Let \(T\) be positive and let \(\mathcal C\) be a nonempty finite cohort set. Fix cohort shares \(\pi_g\in(0,1)\), baseline levels \(\bar b_g>0\), time effects \(\gamma_t\), and a proportional-effect array \(\delta\). Assume:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Rank.)} The collapsed design satisfies Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:collapsed-design-rank}.
\item \textbf{(Cell.)} The cohort \(k\) belongs to \(\mathcal C\), the period \(s\in\{1,\ldots,T\}\), and the cohort-period cell is treated: \(D_{ks}=1\).
\end{itemize}
Write
\[
\widetilde W_{ks}(\delta)
\]
for the pseudo-true \(q_{gt}\mu^\star_{gt}(\delta)\)-weighted FWL residual of the treatment indicator in cell \((k,s)\), and define
\[
\mathcal E(\delta)
=
\sum_{g\in\mathcal C}\sum_{t=1}^T
q_{gt}\,\mu^\star_{gt}(\delta)\,
\widetilde W_{gt}(\delta)^2 .
\]
Then \(\mathcal E(\delta)>0\), and the one-coordinate path
\[
x\mapsto \beta^\star\!\bigl(\delta \text{ with } \delta_{ks}\text{ replaced by }x\bigr)
\]
is differentiable at \(x=\delta_{ks}\) with derivative
\[
\frac{
q_{ks}\,B_{ks}\exp(\delta_{ks})\,\widetilde W_{ks}(\delta)
}{
\mathcal E(\delta)
}.
\]
Consequently,
\[
\frac{\partial \beta^\star(\delta)}{\partial \delta_{ks}}<0
\iff
\widetilde W_{ks}(\delta)<0,\qquad
\frac{\partial \beta^\star(\delta)}{\partial \delta_{ks}}=0
\iff
\widetilde W_{ks}(\delta)=0,
\]
and
\[
\frac{\partial \beta^\star(\delta)}{\partial \delta_{ks}}>0
\iff
\widetilde W_{ks}(\delta)>0.
\]

4465ced0e2c06449cd528eb8c0afd1c6db6eec81.title: Homogeneous Effect Reduction
4465ced0e2c06449cd528eb8c0afd1c6db6eec81.body:
Fix $T$, a finite cohort set $\mathcal C$, sampling laws $\{\mathcal P_N\}_N$, potential outcomes $Y_{it}(d)$, positive unit baselines $b_{iN}$, cohort shares $\pi_g\in(0,1)$, positive limiting cohort baselines $\bar b_g$, untreated time components $\gamma_{t0}$, cohort-time log effects $\delta_{gt}$, and a scalar $\delta_0$. Suppose that
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Untreated means.)} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:unit-untreated-exponential-mean} holds.
\item \textbf{(Collapsed rank.)} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:collapsed-design-rank} holds for $\mathcal C$ and $\{\pi_g\}_g$.
\item \textbf{(Common treated-cell effect.)} For every $g\in\mathcal C$ and every period $t$, if $D_{gt}=1$, then $\delta_{gt}=\delta_0$.
\end{itemize}
Then
\[
\beta^\star(\delta)=\delta_0,
\]
and, for every $g\in\mathcal C$ and every period $t$,
\[
\mu^\star_{gt}(\delta)
=
B_{gt}\exp(D_{gt}\delta_0).
\]

813a966b71e000a2ef68185acfc5bdc46e56e4cb.title: Primitive Sign Frontier
813a966b71e000a2ef68185acfc5bdc46e56e4cb.body:
Fix a panel length \(T\), a finite cohort support \(\mathcal C\), triangular-array sampling laws \(\mathcal P_N\), potential outcomes \(Y_{it}(d)\), deterministic cohort labels \(G_i\), positive unit baselines \(b_{iN}\), limiting shares \(\pi_g\in(0,1)\), positive limiting cohort baselines \(\bar b_g\), untreated time effects \(\gamma_{t0}\), and log proportional effects \(\delta_{gt}\). Suppose:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Horizon and support.)} \(T>0\), \(T\ge 4\), the never-treated cohort belongs to \(\mathcal C\), and every finite supported cohort has adoption date different from the first period.
\item \textbf{(Array support.)} For every \(N\) and every unit \(i\), \(G_i\in\mathcal C\).
\item \textbf{(Share limits.)} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:cohort-share-limit} holds for \((\mathcal C,G_i,\pi_g)\).
\item \textbf{(Untreated means.)} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:unit-untreated-exponential-mean} holds for \((\mathcal P_N,Y_{it}(d),b_{iN},\gamma_{t0})\).
\item \textbf{(Baseline limits.)} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:within-cohort-baseline-limit} holds for \((\mathcal C,G_i,b_{iN},\bar b_g)\).
\item \textbf{(Proportional effects.)} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:proportional-effects} holds for \((\mathcal P_N,Y_{it}(d),G_i,\delta_{gt})\).
\item \textbf{(Rank.)} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:collapsed-design-rank} holds for \((\mathcal C,\pi_g)\).
\item \textbf{(Frontier scope.)} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:multicohort-frontier-scope} holds for \((T,\mathcal C)\).
\item \textbf{(Positive effects.)} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:strict-positive-effects} holds for \((\mathcal C,\delta_{gt})\).
\end{itemize}
Define
\[
h_{gt}
=
\pi_g\,\bar b_g\exp(\gamma_{t0})\exp(D_{gt}\delta_{gt}),
\qquad
R_g=\sum_{t=1}^T h_{gt},
\qquad
C_t=\sum_{g\in\mathcal C} h_{gt},
\]
\[
M=\sum_{g\in\mathcal C}R_g,
\qquad
A=\sum_{g\in\mathcal C}\sum_{t=1}^T D_{gt}h_{gt},
\qquad
\Phi
=
MA-\sum_{g\in\mathcal C}\sum_{t=1}^T D_{gt}R_gC_t .
\]
Then
\[
\beta^\star(\delta)<0\iff \Phi<0,\qquad
\beta^\star(\delta)=0\iff \Phi=0,\qquad
0<\beta^\star(\delta)\iff 0<\Phi .
\]
Moreover, the primitive tuple induced by \((\pi_g,\bar b_g,\gamma_{t0},\delta_{gt})\) belongs to \(\mathcal R_T\) if and only if \(\Phi<0\).

For every \(x>1\) and \(y>1\), in the four-period support \(\{2,3,4,\infty\}\) with equal shares, unit limiting baselines, zero untreated time effects, multiplier \(x\) on treated cells other than \((2,4)\), and multiplier \(y\) at \((2,4)\),
\[
\Phi=\frac{5x^2+12x-2y-15}{16}.
\]
Also,
\[
\frac{5(101/100)^2+12(101/100)-15}{2}=\frac{4441}{4000},
\]
and the explicit witness \(W_4\) has
\[
\Phi=-\frac{11559}{32000}.
\]

Let
\[
m_{gt}=\bar b_g\exp(\gamma_{t0})\exp(D_{gt}\delta_{gt}),
\qquad
H=\{(g,t):g\in\mathcal C,\ D_{gt}=1\}.
\]
For every \((g,t)\in H\),
\[
B^{obs}_{gt}=B_{gt},\qquad
\tau_{gt}=\exp(\delta_{gt})-1,\qquad
\omega_{gt}>0.
\]
The counterfactual-share weights sum to one:
\[
\sum_{(g,t)\in H}\omega_{gt}=1.
\]
The counterfactual-share PTT satisfies
\[
PTT
=
\frac{\sum_{(g,t)\in H} q_{gt}m_{gt}}
{\sum_{(g,t)\in H} q_{gt}B^{obs}_{gt}}
-1.
\]
Finally,
\[
\Phi<0
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
\beta^\star(\delta)<0<PTT .
\]

bf37104b2bcdc6da0f5fc989a84f75c301c175bb.title: Positive Cohort Shares
bf37104b2bcdc6da0f5fc989a84f75c301c175bb.body:
For every \(N\geq |\mathcal C|\) and every \(g\in\mathcal C\), the cohort size \(n_{gN}\) is positive, and
\[
\frac{n_{gN}}{N}\to \pi_g\in(0,1).
\]

b24bf8fd82d88d07262ea99acd1a8f41af63d61b.title: Exponential Untreated Mean
b24bf8fd82d88d07262ea99acd1a8f41af63d61b.body:
For every unit \(i\) and period \(t\),
\[
E_{\mathcal P_N}\!\left[Y_{it}(0)\right]=b_{iN}\exp(\gamma_{t0}),
\]
with the normalization \(\gamma_{10}=0\).

de8218a7dd21698b7ef6df82a3837baafe7ca1b7.title: Cohort Baseline Limit
de8218a7dd21698b7ef6df82a3837baafe7ca1b7.body:
For every \(g\in\mathcal C\), the deterministic triangular-array average
\[
\bar b_{gN}=n_{gN}^{-1}\sum_{i\in I_{gN}} b_{iN}
\]
converges to \(\bar b_g\in(0,\infty)\).

11c2243cdd8d725483dfd5d47d7d6eabac54c67d.title: Proportional Treatment Effects
11c2243cdd8d725483dfd5d47d7d6eabac54c67d.body:
For every unit \(i\) and period \(t\) such that \(D_{it}=1\),
\[
E_{\mathcal P_N}\!\left[Y_{it}(1)\right]
=
E_{\mathcal P_N}\!\left[Y_{it}(0)\right]\exp(\delta_{G_i,t}).
\]

1c419f53fdc4c8cb407dd9098126752125f82835.title: Collapsed Design Rank
1c419f53fdc4c8cb407dd9098126752125f82835.body:
The collapsed second-moment matrix
\[
\sum_{g\in\mathcal C}\sum_{t=1}^T q_{gt} r_{gt} r_{gt}'
\]
is positive definite.

d742b7f61ed31d85c13fbba649c715aae6255042.title: Unit-FE Population Projection \(\beta_N^\star(\delta)\)
d742b7f61ed31d85c13fbba649c715aae6255042.body:
For a given effect profile \(\delta\), let \(\theta_N\in\mathbb R^{N+T}\) maximize
\[
L_N(\theta_N;\delta)
=
\frac{1}{NT}
\sum_{i=1}^N\sum_{t=1}^T
\left\{
m_{itN}(\delta)\,v_{itN}'\theta_N
-
\exp\!\left(v_{itN}'\theta_N\right)
\right\}.
\]
The finite-array population projection coefficient \(\beta_N^\star(\delta)\) is the last coordinate of this maximizer.

1469080c8e6a63c679f2c2dc8c53db0c2b57f2f2.title: Collapsed Projection \(\theta^\star(\delta)\)
1469080c8e6a63c679f2c2dc8c53db0c2b57f2f2.body:
For a given effect profile \(\delta\), define
\[
\theta^\star(\delta)
=
\operatorname*{arg\,max}_{\theta\in\mathbb R^{|\mathcal C|+T}}
L(\theta;\delta),
\]
where
\[
L(\theta;\delta)
=
\sum_{g\in\mathcal C}\sum_{t=1}^T
q_{gt}
\left\{
m_{gt}(\delta)\,r_{gt}'\theta
-
\exp\!\left(r_{gt}'\theta\right)
\right\}.
\]
The limiting collapsed treatment coefficient \(\beta^\star(\delta)\) is the last coordinate of \(\theta^\star(\delta)\), and the fitted collapsed mean is
\[
\mu^\star_{gt}(\delta)
=
\exp\!\left(r_{gt}'\theta^\star(\delta)\right).
\]

12f2ca0b7a2b2af0538f0c7cb052c8eede52b5dc.title: Sample Unit-FE PPML \(\hat\beta_N\)
12f2ca0b7a2b2af0538f0c7cb052c8eede52b5dc.body:
Let \(A_N\) be the argmax set of
\[
\sum_{i=1}^N\sum_{t=1}^T
\left\{
Y_{it}\,v_{itN}'\theta_N
-
\exp\!\left(v_{itN}'\theta_N\right)
\right\}
\]
over unit-effect coordinates in \(\mathbb R\cup\{-\infty\}\), time-effect coordinates in \(\mathbb R\) subject to the period-\(1\) normalization, and \(\beta\in\mathbb R\), with the convention \(\exp(-\infty)=0\).

If \(A_N\) contains a point with finite \(\beta\), then \(\hat\beta_N\) is the \(\beta\)-coordinate selected after choosing, among the finite time-effect and \(\beta\) coordinates, the point with smallest Euclidean norm. If \(A_N\) contains no point with finite \(\beta\), then \(\hat\beta_N=0\).

0b6756e4ed4fce5b8aebbb06eef8f480251ea638.title: Weighted FWL Residual \(\widetilde W_{gt}(\delta)\)
0b6756e4ed4fce5b8aebbb06eef8f480251ea638.body:
For a given effect profile \(\delta\), define
\[
\rho^\star(\delta)
=
\operatorname*{arg\,min}_{\rho\in\mathbb R^{|\mathcal C|+T-1}}
\sum_{g\in\mathcal C}\sum_{t=1}^T
q_{gt}\,\mu^\star_{gt}(\delta)
\left(D_{gt}-X_{gt}'\rho\right)^2 .
\]
The weighted Frisch--Waugh--Lovell residualized treatment is
\[
\widetilde W_{gt}(\delta)
=
D_{gt}-X_{gt}'\rho^\star(\delta).
\]

a49c57eff1f131d500a407bf8165d79b645ac4e9.title: Sign-Reversal Region \(\mathcal R_T\)
a49c57eff1f131d500a407bf8165d79b645ac4e9.body:
The global sign-reversal region \(\mathcal R_T\) is the set of all triples
\[
\left((\pi_g)_{g\in\mathcal C},(B_{gt})_{g\in\mathcal C,\,1\le t\le T},(\delta_{gt})_{(g,t):D_{gt}=1}\right)
\]
such that:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Cohorts.)} \(\mathcal C\) contains \(\{2,3,4,\infty\}\).
\item \textbf{(Untreated means.)} For every \(g\in\mathcal C\) and \(t=1,\ldots,T\),
\[
B_{gt}=\bar b_g\exp(\gamma_{t0}).
\]
\item \textbf{(Positive treated-cell effects.)} For every \((g,t)\) with \(D_{gt}=1\),
\[
\delta_{gt}>0.
\]
\item \textbf{(Full rank.)} The matrix
\[
\sum_{g\in\mathcal C}\sum_{t=1}^T q_{gt}r_{gt}r_{gt}'
\]
is positive definite.
\item \textbf{(Negative limiting coefficient.)} The limiting collapsed treatment coefficient satisfies
\[
\beta^\star(\delta)<0.
\]
\end{itemize}

e93660da031efe353df915551fe92701df63f379.title: Four-cohort witness $W_4$
e93660da031efe353df915551fe92701df63f379.body:
The triangular array $W_4$ is the cofinal subsequence indexed by $N=4m$, $m=1,2,\ldots$, with
\[
T=4,\qquad
\mathcal C=\{2,3,4,\infty\},
\]
\[
n_{2N}=n_{3N}=n_{4N}=n_{\infty N}=m,
\qquad
b_{iN}=1\ \text{for every }i,
\qquad
\gamma_{t0}=0\ \text{for every }t.
\]
For the proportional-effect log multipliers,
\[
\delta_{gt}=\log(101/100)
\]
for every treated cohort-time cell except $(g,t)=(2,4)$, and
\[
\delta_{2,4}=\log 4.
\]

f5b0269c6aa72dfc8c8415509a85a1442313197b.title: Frontier elimination characterization
f5b0269c6aa72dfc8c8415509a85a1442313197b.body:
The frontier elimination characterization is obtained from the limiting collapsed score equations
\[
\sum_{g,t} q_{gt}X_{gt}\{m_{gt}(\delta)-\mu^\star_{gt}(\delta)\}=0
\]
and
\[
\sum_{g,t} q_{gt}D_{gt}\{m_{gt}(\delta)-\mu^\star_{gt}(\delta)\}=0.
\]
After eliminating the intercept, cohort, and time multipliers through the fitted row and column margins, it characterizes the zero-level set
\[
\beta^\star(\delta)=0
\]
in terms of the limiting cohort shares
\[
\pi_g=\lim_{N\to\infty} n_{gN}/N,
\]
the untreated cohort-time means $B_{gt}$, and the proportional-effect log multipliers $\delta_{gt}$.

755ac53b69c7aef7e161c78de6a5a48bb04509bd.title: Cohort share limits
755ac53b69c7aef7e161c78de6a5a48bb04509bd.body:
For every \(N\ge |\mathcal C|\) and every \(g\in\mathcal C\),
\[
n_{gN}>0
\quad\text{and}\quad
\frac{n_{gN}}{N}\to \pi_g\in(0,1).
\]

1aa1cd01ffccde4e8f7e2798856bfcd5781a2aa4.title: Untreated exponential mean
1aa1cd01ffccde4e8f7e2798856bfcd5781a2aa4.body:
For every unit \(i=1,\ldots,N\) and period \(t=1,\ldots,T\),
\[
E_{\mathcal P_N}\!\left[Y_{it}(0)\right]
=
b_{iN}\exp(\gamma_{t0}),
\]
with the normalization \(\gamma_{10}=0\).

9d3176def81040be99f98f0337e4b04217dc41d7.title: Within-cohort baseline limits
9d3176def81040be99f98f0337e4b04217dc41d7.body:
For every \(g\in\mathcal C\), the deterministic triangular-array average
\[
\bar b_{gN}
=
n_{gN}^{-1}\sum_{i\in I_{gN}} b_{iN}
\]
satisfies
\[
\bar b_{gN}\to \bar b_g\in(0,\infty).
\]

509eb90dade7b9e8aa71327c6ec7696c67ee0441.title: Proportional treatment effects
509eb90dade7b9e8aa71327c6ec7696c67ee0441.body:
For every unit \(i=1,\ldots,N\) and period \(t=1,\ldots,T\) with \(D_{it}=1\),
\[
E_{\mathcal P_N}\!\left[Y_{it}(1)\right]
=
E_{\mathcal P_N}\!\left[Y_{it}(0)\right]\exp(\delta_{G_i t}).
\]

c3ff47c24120f7744a27e09a4aaebceb83830571.title: Collapsed design rank
c3ff47c24120f7744a27e09a4aaebceb83830571.body:
The collapsed second-moment matrix
\[
\sum_{g\in\mathcal C}\sum_{t=1}^T q_{gt} r_{gt} r_{gt}'
\]
is positive definite.

9c77a1ada11692aa147caf34497702bd99abd8da.title: Unit-FE Projection \(\beta_N^\star(\delta)\)
9c77a1ada11692aa147caf34497702bd99abd8da.body:
Define \(\beta_N^\star(\delta)\) as the last coordinate of a maximizer over \(\theta_N\in\mathbb R^{N+T}\) of
\[
L_N(\theta_N;\delta)
=
(NT)^{-1}\sum_{i=1}^N\sum_{t=1}^T
\left\{
m_{itN}(\delta)\,v_{itN}'\theta_N-\exp(v_{itN}'\theta_N)
\right\}.
\]

9affcc35b5e003674d009e0a611aa308e0df9510.title: Collapsed Projection \(\theta^\star(\delta)\)
9affcc35b5e003674d009e0a611aa308e0df9510.body:
Define
\[
\theta^\star(\delta)\in
\arg\max_{\theta\in\mathbb R^{|\mathcal C|+T}} L(\theta;\delta),
\]
where
\[
L(\theta;\delta)
=
\sum_{g\in\mathcal C}\sum_{t=1}^T
q_{gt}
\left\{
m_{gt}(\delta)\,r_{gt}'\theta-\exp(r_{gt}'\theta)
\right\}.
\]
The coefficient \(\beta^\star(\delta)\) is the last coordinate of \(\theta^\star(\delta)\), and the fitted collapsed mean is
\[
\mu^\star_{gt}(\delta)=\exp\{r_{gt}'\theta^\star(\delta)\}.
\]

7a0b05b9f195ba79ded26d6cf9c42f18e680aef7.title: Sample PPML Coefficient \(\hat\beta_N\)
7a0b05b9f195ba79ded26d6cf9c42f18e680aef7.body:
Let \(A_N\) be the argmax set of
\[
\sum_{i=1}^N\sum_{t=1}^T
\left\{
Y_{it}\,v_{itN}'\theta_N-\exp(v_{itN}'\theta_N)
\right\}
\]
over unit-effect coordinates in \(\mathbb R\cup\{-\infty\}\), time-effect coordinates in \(\mathbb R\) satisfying the period-1 normalization, and \(\beta\in\mathbb R\), with the convention \(\exp(-\infty)=0\). If \(A_N\) contains a point with finite \(\beta\), then \(\hat\beta_N\) is the \(\beta\)-coordinate obtained after selecting, among the finite time-effect and \(\beta\) coordinates in \(A_N\), the vector of smallest Euclidean norm. If \(A_N\) contains no point with finite \(\beta\), set \(\hat\beta_N=0\).

7aaf9eaca2b48d38db91579bd5cd8462a180763c.title: Weighted Residual \(\widetilde W_{gt}(\delta)\)
7aaf9eaca2b48d38db91579bd5cd8462a180763c.body:
Define
\[
\rho^\star(\delta)
\in
\arg\min_{\rho\in\mathbb R^{|\mathcal C|+T-1}}
\sum_{g\in\mathcal C}\sum_{t=1}^T
q_{gt}\,\mu^\star_{gt}(\delta)\{D_{gt}-X_{gt}'\rho\}^2.
\]
The weighted Frisch--Waugh--Lovell residualized treatment is
\[
\widetilde W_{gt}(\delta)=D_{gt}-X_{gt}'\rho^\star(\delta).
\]

21a2c59f295c90e155c6eb21aea024ede321c8fa.title: Sign-Reversal Region \(\mathcal R_T\)
21a2c59f295c90e155c6eb21aea024ede321c8fa.body:
Define \(\mathcal R_T\) as the set of all triples
\[
\left((\pi_g)_{g\in\mathcal C},(B_{gt})_{g\in\mathcal C,\ 1\le t\le T},(\delta_{gt})_{(g,t):D_{gt}=1}\right)
\]
such that \(\mathcal C\) contains \(\{2,3,4,\infty\}\), \(B_{gt}=\bar b_g\exp(\gamma_{t0})\), \(\delta_{gt}>0\) whenever \(D_{gt}=1\), \(\sum_{g,t}q_{gt}r_{gt}r_{gt}'\) is positive definite, and
\[
\beta^\star(\delta)<0.
\]

7e222837d4123db3a7f0c8745980f2b82d9dcabf.title: Four-cohort witness $W_4$
7e222837d4123db3a7f0c8745980f2b82d9dcabf.body:
Let $W_4$ be the cofinal triangular subsequence indexed by $N=4m$, $m=1,2,\ldots$, with $T=4$ and
\[
\mathcal C=\{2,3,4,\infty\}.
\]
For each such $N$,
\[
n_{2N}=n_{3N}=n_{4N}=n_{\infty N}=m,
\]
$b_{iN}=1$ for every $i$, and $\gamma_{t0}=0$ for every $t$. The treatment effects satisfy
\[
\delta_{gt}=\log(101/100)
\]
for every treated cell $(g,t)\ne(2,4)$, while
\[
\delta_{2,4}=\log(4).
\]

c2b7885d0947d2ed6528048b385be5742101d58a.title: Frontier for $\beta^\star(\delta)=0$
c2b7885d0947d2ed6528048b385be5742101d58a.body:
The frontier-elimination handle is the characterization of the zero-level set
\[
\beta^\star(\delta)=0
\]
in terms of $\pi_g=\lim_{N\to\infty} n_{gN}/N$, $B_{gt}$, and $\delta_{gt}$ obtained from the limiting collapsed score equations
\[
\sum_{g\in\mathcal C}\sum_{t=1}^T
q_{gt}X_{gt}\{m_{gt}(\delta)-\mu^\star_{gt}(\delta)\}=0
\]
and
\[
\sum_{g\in\mathcal C}\sum_{t=1}^T
q_{gt}D_{gt}\{m_{gt}(\delta)-\mu^\star_{gt}(\delta)\}=0,
\]
with the intercept, cohort, and time multipliers eliminated through their fitted row and column margins.

bec2feaad40780a5326ba83444dd43619fa44cde.title: Primitive Global Frontier
bec2feaad40780a5326ba83444dd43619fa44cde.body:
Fix a panel length \(T\), a finite cohort support \(\mathcal C\), triangular-array sampling laws \(\mathcal P_N\), potential outcomes \(Y_{it}(d)\), deterministic cohort labels \(G_i\), positive unit baselines \(b_{iN}\), limiting shares \(\pi_g\in(0,1)\), positive limiting cohort baselines \(\bar b_g\), untreated time effects \(\gamma_{t0}\), and log proportional effects \(\delta_{gt}\). Suppose:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Horizon and support.)} \(T>0\), \(T\ge 4\), the never-treated cohort belongs to \(\mathcal C\), and every finite supported cohort has adoption date different from the first period.
\item \textbf{(Array support.)} For every \(N\) and every unit \(i\), \(G_i\in\mathcal C\).
\item \textbf{(Share limits.)} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:cohort-share-limit} holds for \((\mathcal C,G_i,\pi_g)\).
\item \textbf{(Untreated means.)} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:unit-untreated-exponential-mean} holds for \((\mathcal P_N,Y_{it}(d),b_{iN},\gamma_{t0})\).
\item \textbf{(Baseline limits.)} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:within-cohort-baseline-limit} holds for \((\mathcal C,G_i,b_{iN},\bar b_g)\).
\item \textbf{(Proportional effects.)} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:proportional-effects} holds for \((\mathcal P_N,Y_{it}(d),G_i,\delta_{gt})\).
\item \textbf{(Rank.)} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:collapsed-design-rank} holds for \((\mathcal C,\pi_g)\).
\item \textbf{(Frontier scope.)} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:multicohort-frontier-scope} holds for \((T,\mathcal C)\).
\item \textbf{(Positive effects.)} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:strict-positive-effects} holds for \((\mathcal C,\delta_{gt})\).
\end{itemize}
Define
\[
h_{gt}
=
\pi_g\,\bar b_g\exp(\gamma_{t0})\exp(D_{gt}\delta_{gt}),
\qquad
R_g=\sum_{t=1}^T h_{gt},
\qquad
C_t=\sum_{g\in\mathcal C} h_{gt},
\]
\[
M=\sum_{g\in\mathcal C}R_g,
\qquad
A=\sum_{g\in\mathcal C}\sum_{t=1}^T D_{gt}h_{gt},
\qquad
\Phi
=
MA-\sum_{g\in\mathcal C}\sum_{t=1}^T D_{gt}R_gC_t .
\]
Then
\[
\beta^\star(\delta)<0\iff \Phi<0,\qquad
\beta^\star(\delta)=0\iff \Phi=0,\qquad
0<\beta^\star(\delta)\iff 0<\Phi .
\]
Moreover, the primitive tuple induced by \((\pi_g,\bar b_g,\gamma_{t0},\delta_{gt})\) belongs to \(\mathcal R_T\) if and only if \(\Phi<0\).

For every \(x>1\) and \(y>1\), in the four-period support \(\{2,3,4,\infty\}\) with equal shares, unit limiting baselines, zero untreated time effects, multiplier \(x\) on treated cells other than \((2,4)\), and multiplier \(y\) at \((2,4)\),
\[
\Phi=\frac{5x^2+12x-2y-15}{16}.
\]
Also,
\[
\frac{5(101/100)^2+12(101/100)-15}{2}=\frac{4441}{4000},
\]
and the explicit witness \(W_4\) has
\[
\Phi=-\frac{11559}{32000}.
\]

Let
\[
m_{gt}=\bar b_g\exp(\gamma_{t0})\exp(D_{gt}\delta_{gt}),
\qquad
H=\{(g,t):g\in\mathcal C,\ D_{gt}=1\}.
\]
For every \((g,t)\in H\),
\[
B^{obs}_{gt}=B_{gt},\qquad
\tau_{gt}=\exp(\delta_{gt})-1,\qquad
\omega_{gt}>0.
\]
The counterfactual-share weights sum to one:
\[
\sum_{(g,t)\in H}\omega_{gt}=1.
\]
The counterfactual-share PTT satisfies
\[
PTT
=
\frac{\sum_{(g,t)\in H} q_{gt}m_{gt}}
{\sum_{(g,t)\in H} q_{gt}B^{obs}_{gt}}
-1.
\]
Finally,
\[
\Phi<0
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
\beta^\star(\delta)<0<PTT .
\]

ee267e469e59c2591d0c1497f7948d653fd33553.title: Counterfactual-share weights $\omega_{gt}$
ee267e469e59c2591d0c1497f7948d653fd33553.body:
The counterfactual-share weights on treated cells are the quantities $\omega_{gt}$ defined as follows. Let
\[
H=\{(g,t):D_{gt}=1\},\qquad
B^{obs}_{gt}=\frac{m_{g1}m_{\infty t}}{m_{\infty 1}},
\qquad
Z=\sum_{(k,s)\in H}q_{ks}B^{obs}_{ks}.
\]
For each treated cell $(g,t)\in H$,
\[
\omega_{gt}=\frac{q_{gt}B^{obs}_{gt}}{Z}.
\]

ec7048fd1677e1a32cc4fb1cc89a30ffbc75944e.title: Limiting observed cohort mean $m_{gt}(\delta)$
ec7048fd1677e1a32cc4fb1cc89a30ffbc75944e.body:
For each cohort $g\in\mathcal C$ and period $t\in\{1,\ldots,T\}$, the limiting observed cohort mean under effects $\delta$ is
\[
m_{gt}(\delta)=B_{gt}\exp(D_{gt}\delta_{gt}),
\]
where
\[
B_{gt}=\bar b_g\exp(\gamma_{t0}),
\]
and $D_{gt}$ is the cohort-time treatment indicator.

3ee667fa976749e55e58bb6bc420cc89e54ddfe9.title: Finite-array collapsed criterion $L_{Nc}(\theta;\delta)$
3ee667fa976749e55e58bb6bc420cc89e54ddfe9.body:
For $\theta\in\mathbb R^{|\mathcal C|+T}$, the finite-array collapsed Poisson pseudo-criterion is
\[
L_{Nc}(\theta;\delta)
=
\sum_{g\in\mathcal C}\sum_{t=1}^T
q_{gtN}
\left\{
m_{gtN}(\delta)r_{gt}'\theta-\exp(r_{gt}'\theta)
\right\},
\]
where
\[
q_{gtN}=\frac{n_{gN}}{NT},\qquad
m_{gtN}(\delta)=B_{gtN}\exp(D_{gt}\delta_{gt}),
\]
\[
B_{gtN}=\bar b_{gN}\exp(\gamma_{t0}),
\qquad
\bar b_{gN}=n_{gN}^{-1}\sum_{i\in I_{gN}} b_{iN},
\]
and $r_{gt}$ is the collapsed regressor containing an intercept, cohort effects, time effects, and treatment, with the treatment coordinate last.

aed00163836b06d76e7e506abef7f8497d5d4056.title: Sharp PPML Sign Formula
aed00163836b06d76e7e506abef7f8497d5d4056.body:
Let \(T>0\) and let \(\mathcal C\) be a nonempty finite cohort set. Fix cohort shares \(\pi_g\in(0,1)\), baseline levels \(\bar b_g>0\), untreated time components \(\gamma_{t0}\), and a proportional-effect array \(\delta\). Assume:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Rank.)} The collapsed design satisfies Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:collapsed-design-rank}.
\item \textbf{(Cell.)} The cohort \(k\) belongs to \(\mathcal C\), the period \(s\in\{1,\ldots,T\}\), and the cohort-period cell is treated: \(D_{ks}=1\).
\end{itemize}
Write \(\widetilde W_{ks}(\delta)\) for the pseudo-true \(q_{gt}\mu^\star_{gt}(\delta)\)-weighted FWL residual of the treatment indicator in cell \((k,s)\), and define
\[
\mathcal E(\delta)
=
\sum_{g\in\mathcal C}\sum_{t=1}^T
q_{gt}\,\mu^\star_{gt}(\delta)\,
\widetilde W_{gt}(\delta)^2 .
\]
Then \(\mathcal E(\delta)>0\), and the one-coordinate path
\[
x\mapsto \beta^\star\!\bigl(\delta \text{ with } \delta_{ks}\text{ replaced by }x\bigr)
\]
is differentiable at \(x=\delta_{ks}\) with derivative
\[
\frac{
q_{ks}\,B_{ks}\exp(\delta_{ks})\,\widetilde W_{ks}(\delta)
}{
\mathcal E(\delta)
}.
\]
Consequently,
\[
\frac{\partial \beta^\star(\delta)}{\partial \delta_{ks}}<0
\iff
\widetilde W_{ks}(\delta)<0,\qquad
\frac{\partial \beta^\star(\delta)}{\partial \delta_{ks}}=0
\iff
\widetilde W_{ks}(\delta)=0,
\]
and
\[
\frac{\partial \beta^\star(\delta)}{\partial \delta_{ks}}>0
\iff
\widetilde W_{ks}(\delta)>0.
\]

4108073da2008a60f5c9e36c715bdfc9acce08e8.title: Frontier-elimination polynomial $\Phi(\delta)$
4108073da2008a60f5c9e36c715bdfc9acce08e8.body:
Define
\[
\Phi(\delta)
=
M A
-
\sum_{g,t} D_{gt} R_g C_t,
\]
where
\[
h_{gt}
=
\pi_g B_{gt}\exp(D_{gt}\delta_{gt}),\qquad
R_g=\sum_t h_{gt},\qquad
C_t=\sum_g h_{gt},
\]
and
\[
M=\sum_g R_g,\qquad
A=\sum_{g,t}D_{gt}h_{gt}.
\]
The relation between $\Phi(\delta)$ and $\beta^\star(\delta)$ is stated separately.

0c576a4eccd1340c5c0854a13b15ed29c4afe2d8.title: Four-Cohort Sign Reversal
0c576a4eccd1340c5c0854a13b15ed29c4afe2d8.body:
The four-cohort witness \(W_4\) is constructed with cohort support
\(\mathcal C=\{2,3,4,\infty\}\), equal shares \(\pi_g=1/4\), limiting baselines
\(\bar b_g=1\), untreated time component \(\gamma_{t0}=0\), and proportional-effect
log multipliers
\[
\delta_{gt}=
\begin{cases}
\log 4, & (g,t)=(2,4),\\
\log(101/100), & D_{gt}=1 \text{ and } (g,t)\neq(2,4),\\
0, & D_{gt}=0 .
\end{cases}
\]
Then:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Region membership.)} The primitive \(W_4\) belongs to the \(T=4\) specialization of the sign-reversal region \(\mathcal R_T\) in Definition~\ref{obj:def:global-sign-reversal-region}.
\item \textbf{(Positive effects.)} Every treated supported cell has a strictly positive proportional-effect log multiplier: if \(g\in\mathcal C\), \(t\in\{1,2,3,4\}\), and \(D_{gt}=1\), then \(\delta_{gt}>0\).
\item \textbf{(Unique largest treated effect.)} The treated cell \((2,4)\) has the strictly largest proportional-effect log multiplier: if \(g\in\mathcal C\), \(t\in\{1,2,3,4\}\), \(D_{gt}=1\), and \((g,t)\neq(2,4)\), then \(\delta_{gt}<\delta_{2,4}\).
\item \textbf{(Weighted FWL residuals.)} At the no-effect vector, the weighted FWL residual \(\widetilde W_{gt}\) in Definition~\ref{obj:def:weighted-fwl-residual} equals
\[
\frac{1}{8}
\begin{pmatrix}
-3 & 3 & 1 & -1\\
-1 & -3 & 3 & 1\\
1 & -1 & -3 & 3\\
3 & 1 & -1 & -3
\end{pmatrix},
\]
with rows \(g=2,3,4,\infty\) and columns \(t=1,2,3,4\). In particular, \(\widetilde W_{2,4}=-1/8\).
\item \textbf{(Negative late-cell derivative at zero.)} Holding all other effects at zero,
\[
\left.\frac{d}{dx}\,
\beta^\star\!\left(\delta_{2,4}=x,\ \delta_{gt}=0\text{ for }(g,t)\neq(2,4)\right)
\right|_{x=0}
=-\frac{1}{10}.
\]
\item \textbf{(Local negative derivative under positive effects.)} There exists \(\varepsilon>0\) such that, for every effect vector \(\delta'\) satisfying \(|\delta'_{gt}|<\varepsilon\) for all \(g\in\mathcal C\) and \(t\in\{1,2,3,4\}\), if \(\delta'\) has strictly positive treated effects in the sense of Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:strict-positive-effects}, then
\[
\left.
\frac{d}{dx}\,
\beta^\star\!\left(\delta' \text{ with } \delta'_{2,4}\text{ replaced by }x\right)
\right|_{x=\delta'_{2,4}}
<0 .
\]
\end{itemize}

899314ef5f3b192622ee88bdebfece26055a77e8.title: Four-Cohort Sign Reversal
899314ef5f3b192622ee88bdebfece26055a77e8.body:
The four-cohort witness \(W_4\) is constructed with cohort support
\(\mathcal C=\{2,3,4,\infty\}\), equal shares \(\pi_g=1/4\), limiting baselines
\(\bar b_g=1\), untreated time component \(\gamma_{t0}=0\), and proportional-effect
log multipliers
\[
\delta_{gt}=
\begin{cases}
\log 4, & (g,t)=(2,4),\\
\log(101/100), & D_{gt}=1 \text{ and } (g,t)\neq(2,4),\\
0, & D_{gt}=0 .
\end{cases}
\]
Then:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Region membership.)} The primitive \(W_4\), restricted to the supported cohort-time cells, belongs to the four-period sign-reversal region \(\mathcal R_4\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:global-sign-reversal-region}.
\item \textbf{(Positive treated effects.)} For every \(g\in\mathcal C\) and \(t=1,\ldots,4\), if \(D_{gt}=1\), then \(\delta_{gt}>0\).
\item \textbf{(Largest late cell.)} For every \(g\in\mathcal C\) and \(t=1,\ldots,4\), if \(D_{gt}=1\) and \((g,t)\neq(2,4)\), then \(\delta_{gt}<\delta_{2,4}\).
\item \textbf{(Weighted FWL residuals.)} At the no-effect vector, the weighted FWL residual \(\widetilde W_{gt}\) in Definition~\ref{obj:def:weighted-fwl-residual} equals
\[
\frac{1}{8}
\begin{pmatrix}
-3 & 3 & 1 & -1\\
-1 & -3 & 3 & 1\\
1 & -1 & -3 & 3\\
3 & 1 & -1 & -3
\end{pmatrix},
\]
with rows \(g=2,3,4,\infty\) and columns \(t=1,2,3,4\). In particular,
\[
\widetilde W_{2,4}=-\frac{1}{8}.
\]
\item \textbf{(Zero-effect derivative.)} Holding all other effects at zero,
\[
\left.
\frac{d}{dx}\,
\beta^\star\!\left(\delta_{2,4}=x,\ \delta_{gt}=0\text{ for }(g,t)\neq(2,4)\right)
\right|_{x=0}
=-\frac{1}{10}.
\]
\item \textbf{(Local negative derivative.)} There exists \(\varepsilon>0\) such that, for every effect vector \(\delta'\) with
\[
|\delta'_{gt}|<\varepsilon
\qquad\text{for all }g\in\mathcal C,\ t=1,\ldots,4,
\]
if \(\delta'\) has strictly positive treated effects in the sense of Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:strict-positive-effects}, then
\[
\left.
\frac{d}{dx}\,
\beta^\star\!\left(\delta' \text{ with } \delta'_{2,4}\text{ replaced by }x\right)
\right|_{x=\delta'_{2,4}}
<0 .
\]
\end{itemize}

f803a0db2345a13af35680b0ce5861d772a36321.title: Four-period sign-reversal region $\mathcal R_4$
f803a0db2345a13af35680b0ce5861d772a36321.body:
The four-period sign-reversal region $\mathcal R_4$ is the set of primitive arrays
\[
\left((\pi_g)_{g\in\{2,3,4,\infty\}},\,
(B_{gt})_{g\in\{2,3,4,\infty\},\,1\le t\le4},\,
(\delta_{gt})_{(g,t):D_{gt}=1}\right)
\]
satisfying the following conditions:

\(\bullet\) \textbf{(Cohort shares.)} For every \(g\in\{2,3,4,\infty\}\), \(\pi_g\in(0,1)\), and
\[
\sum_{g\in\{2,3,4,\infty\}}\pi_g=1.
\]

\(\bullet\) \textbf{(Cell masses.)} For every \(g\in\{2,3,4,\infty\}\) and \(t=1,\ldots,4\),
\[
q_{gt}=\frac{\pi_g}{4}.
\]

\(\bullet\) \textbf{(Untreated means.)} For every \(g\in\{2,3,4,\infty\}\) and \(t=1,\ldots,4\),
\[
B_{gt}=\bar b_g\exp(\gamma_{t0})
\]
for some \(\bar b_g>0\).

\(\bullet\) \textbf{(Positive treatment effects.)} For every treated cell \((g,t)\) with \(D_{gt}=1\),
\[
\delta_{gt}>0.
\]

\(\bullet\) \textbf{(Full rank.)} The matrix
\[
\sum_{g\in\{2,3,4,\infty\}}\sum_{t=1}^4 q_{gt}r_{gt}r_{gt}'
\]
is positive definite.

\(\bullet\) \textbf{(Negative limiting PPML coefficient.)} The treatment coordinate of the limiting collapsed PPML projection satisfies
\[
\beta^\star(\delta)<0.
\]

9870f17f2492a45b1819cfff267cb9909321dd61.title: Four-Cohort Sign Reversal
9870f17f2492a45b1819cfff267cb9909321dd61.body:
The four-cohort witness \(W_4\) is constructed with cohort support
\(\mathcal C=\{2,3,4,\infty\}\), equal shares \(\pi_g=1/4\), limiting baselines
\(\bar b_g=1\), untreated time component \(\gamma_{t0}=0\), and proportional-effect
log multipliers
\[
\delta_{gt}=
\begin{cases}
\log 4, & (g,t)=(2,4),\\
\log(101/100), & D_{gt}=1 \text{ and } (g,t)\neq(2,4),\\
0, & D_{gt}=0 .
\end{cases}
\]
Then:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Region membership.)} The primitive \(W_4\), restricted to the supported cohort-time cells, belongs to the sign-reversal region \(\mathcal R_T\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:global-sign-reversal-region} with \(T=4\), cohort support \(\mathcal C\), and untreated time component \(\gamma_{t0}\).
\item \textbf{(Positive treated effects.)} For every \(g\in\mathcal C\) and \(t=1,\ldots,4\), if \(D_{gt}=1\), then \(\delta_{gt}>0\).
\item \textbf{(Largest late cell.)} For every \(g\in\mathcal C\) and \(t=1,\ldots,4\), if \(D_{gt}=1\) and \((g,t)\neq(2,4)\), then \(\delta_{gt}<\delta_{2,4}\).
\item \textbf{(Weighted FWL residuals.)} At the no-effect vector, the weighted FWL residual \(\widetilde W_{gt}\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:weighted-fwl-residual} equals
\[
\frac{1}{8}
\begin{pmatrix}
-3 & 3 & 1 & -1\\
-1 & -3 & 3 & 1\\
1 & -1 & -3 & 3\\
3 & 1 & -1 & -3
\end{pmatrix},
\]
with rows \(g=2,3,4,\infty\) and columns \(t=1,2,3,4\). In particular,
\[
\widetilde W_{2,4}=-\frac{1}{8}.
\]
\item \textbf{(Zero-effect derivative.)} Holding all other effects at zero,
\[
\left.
\frac{d}{dx}\,
\beta^\star\!\left(\delta_{2,4}=x,\ \delta_{gt}=0\text{ for }(g,t)\neq(2,4)\right)
\right|_{x=0}
=-\frac{1}{10}.
\]
\item \textbf{(Local negative derivative.)} There exists \(\varepsilon>0\) such that, for every effect vector \(\delta'\) satisfying
\[
|\delta'_{gt}|<\varepsilon
\qquad\text{for all }g\in\mathcal C,\ t=1,\ldots,4,
\]
if \(\delta'\) has strictly positive treated effects in the sense of Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:strict-positive-effects}, then
\[
\left.
\frac{d}{dx}\,
\beta^\star\!\left(\delta' \text{ with } \delta'_{2,4}\text{ replaced by }x\right)
\right|_{x=\delta'_{2,4}}
<0 .
\]
\end{itemize}

32ae73ba96e8b90d619af7371cae85a3d73d9652.title: Limiting PPML coefficient $\beta^\star(\delta)$
32ae73ba96e8b90d619af7371cae85a3d73d9652.body:
Define $\beta^\star(\delta)$ as the treatment coordinate, equivalently the last coordinate, of the unique maximizer $\theta^\star(\delta)\in\mathbb R^{|\mathcal C|+T}$ of
\[
L(\theta;\delta)
=
\sum_{g\in\mathcal C}\sum_{t=1}^T
q_{gt}\left\{
m_{gt}(\delta)\, r_{gt}'\theta-\exp(r_{gt}'\theta)
\right\},
\]
where
\[
m_{gt}(\delta)=B_{gt}\exp(D_{gt}\delta_{gt}).
\]

e77ff5a3d4f19b3bc6c65b5f648c1313e8aa8f2c.title: Four-Cohort Sign Reversal
e77ff5a3d4f19b3bc6c65b5f648c1313e8aa8f2c.body:
The four-cohort witness \(W_4\) is constructed with cohort support
\(\mathcal C=\{2,3,4,\infty\}\), equal shares \(\pi_g=1/4\), limiting baselines
\(\bar b_g=1\), untreated time component \(\gamma_{t0}=0\), and proportional-effect
log multipliers
\[
\delta_{gt}=
\begin{cases}
\log 4, & (g,t)=(2,4),\\
\log(101/100), & D_{gt}=1 \text{ and } (g,t)\neq(2,4),\\
0, & D_{gt}=0 .
\end{cases}
\]
Then:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Region membership.)} The primitive \(W_4\) belongs to the global sign-reversal region \(\mathcal R_T\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:global-sign-reversal-region}, specialized to \(T=4\).
\item \textbf{(Positive effects.)} Every treated supported cell has a strictly positive proportional-effect log multiplier: if \(g\in\mathcal C\), \(t\in\{1,2,3,4\}\), and \(D_{gt}=1\), then \(\delta_{gt}>0\).
\item \textbf{(Unique largest treated effect.)} The treated cell \((2,4)\) has the strictly largest proportional-effect log multiplier: if \(g\in\mathcal C\), \(t\in\{1,2,3,4\}\), \(D_{gt}=1\), and \((g,t)\neq(2,4)\), then \(\delta_{gt}<\delta_{2,4}\).
\item \textbf{(Weighted FWL residuals.)} At the no-effect vector, the weighted FWL residual \(\widetilde W_{gt}\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:weighted-fwl-residual} equals
\[
\frac{1}{8}
\begin{pmatrix}
-3 & 3 & 1 & -1\\
-1 & -3 & 3 & 1\\
1 & -1 & -3 & 3\\
3 & 1 & -1 & -3
\end{pmatrix},
\]
with rows \(g=2,3,4,\infty\) and columns \(t=1,2,3,4\). In particular, \(\widetilde W_{2,4}=-1/8\).
\item \textbf{(Negative late-cell derivative at zero.)} Holding all other effects at zero,
\[
\left.\frac{d}{dx}\,
\beta^\star\!\left(\delta_{2,4}=x,\ \delta_{gt}=0\text{ for }(g,t)\neq(2,4)\right)
\right|_{x=0}
=-\frac{1}{10}.
\]
\item \textbf{(Local negative derivative under positive effects.)} There exists \(\varepsilon>0\) such that, for every effect vector \(\delta'\) satisfying \(|\delta'_{gt}|<\varepsilon\) for all \(g\in\mathcal C\) and \(t\in\{1,2,3,4\}\), if \(\delta'\) has strictly positive treated effects in the sense of Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:strict-positive-effects}, then
\[
\left.
\frac{d}{dx}\,
\beta^\star\!\left(\delta' \text{ with } \delta'_{2,4}\text{ replaced by }x\right)
\right|_{x=\delta'_{2,4}}
<0 .
\]
\end{itemize}

69f54c5f5be12d1245039b309f840045ff57ae83.title: Sharp Forbidden Sign
69f54c5f5be12d1245039b309f840045ff57ae83.body:
Let \(T>0\) and let \(\mathcal C\) be a nonempty finite cohort set. Fix cohort shares \(\pi_g\in(0,1)\), baseline levels \(\bar b_g>0\), untreated time effects \(\gamma_{t0}\), and a proportional-effect array \(\delta\). Assume:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Rank.)} The collapsed design satisfies Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:collapsed-design-rank}.
\item \textbf{(Cell.)} The cohort \(k\) belongs to \(\mathcal C\), the period \(s\in\{1,\ldots,T\}\), and the cohort-period cell is treated: \(D_{ks}=1\).
\end{itemize}
Write \(\widetilde W_{gt}(\delta)\) for the pseudo-true \(q_{gt}\mu^\star_{gt}(\delta)\)-weighted FWL residual of the treatment indicator, and define
\[
\mathcal E(\delta)
=
\sum_{g\in\mathcal C}\sum_{t=1}^T
q_{gt}\,\mu^\star_{gt}(\delta)\,
\widetilde W_{gt}(\delta)^2 .
\]
Then \(\mathcal E(\delta)>0\), and the one-coordinate path
\[
x\mapsto \beta^\star\!\bigl(\delta \text{ with } \delta_{ks}\text{ replaced by }x\bigr)
\]
is differentiable at \(x=\delta_{ks}\) with derivative
\[
\frac{
q_{ks}\,B_{ks}\exp(\delta_{ks})\,\widetilde W_{ks}(\delta)
}{
\mathcal E(\delta)
}.
\]
Consequently,
\[
\frac{\partial \beta^\star(\delta)}{\partial \delta_{ks}}<0
\iff
\widetilde W_{ks}(\delta)<0,\qquad
\frac{\partial \beta^\star(\delta)}{\partial \delta_{ks}}=0
\iff
\widetilde W_{ks}(\delta)=0,
\]
and
\[
\frac{\partial \beta^\star(\delta)}{\partial \delta_{ks}}>0
\iff
\widetilde W_{ks}(\delta)>0.
\]

4bf723e5a99fca7db867d3b09290dbb3c67ae83b.title: Four-Cohort Sign Reversal
4bf723e5a99fca7db867d3b09290dbb3c67ae83b.body:
Let \(T=4\), let \(\mathcal C=\{2,3,4,\infty\}\), set \(\pi_g=1/4\) for every \(g\in\mathcal C\), set \(\bar b_g=1\) for every \(g\in\mathcal C\), and set \(\gamma_{t0}=0\) for every \(t=1,\ldots,4\). Define
\[
\delta_{gt}=
\begin{cases}
\log 4, & (g,t)=(2,4),\\
\log(101/100), & D_{gt}=1 \text{ and } (g,t)\neq(2,4),\\
0, & D_{gt}=0 .
\end{cases}
\]
Then:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Region membership.)} The primitive \(W_4\) obtained from these shares, baselines, time effects, and proportional-effect log multipliers belongs to the sign-reversal region \(\mathcal R_4\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:global-sign-reversal-region}.
\item \textbf{(Positive effects.)} For every \(g\in\mathcal C\) and \(t=1,\ldots,4\), if \(D_{gt}=1\), then \(\delta_{gt}>0\).
\item \textbf{(Largest treated effect.)} For every \(g\in\mathcal C\) and \(t=1,\ldots,4\), if \(D_{gt}=1\) and \((g,t)\neq(2,4)\), then \(\delta_{gt}<\delta_{2,4}\).
\item \textbf{(No-effect FWL residuals.)} Write \(\widetilde W_{gt}(0)\) for the weighted FWL residual of Definition~\ref{obj:def:weighted-fwl-residual} computed with all proportional-effect log multipliers set to zero. For every supported cell \((g,t)\),
\[
\widetilde W_{gt}(0)
=
\frac{1}{8}
\begin{pmatrix}
-3 & 3 & 1 & -1\\
-1 & -3 & 3 & 1\\
1 & -1 & -3 & 3\\
3 & 1 & -1 & -3
\end{pmatrix}_{g,t},
\]
where the rows are \(g=2,3,4,\infty\) and the columns are \(t=1,2,3,4\). In particular, \(\widetilde W_{2,4}(0)=-1/8\).
\item \textbf{(Late-cell derivative at zero.)} Holding all other proportional-effect log multipliers equal to zero,
\[
\left.
\frac{d}{dx}\,
\beta^\star\!\left(\delta_{2,4}=x,\ \delta_{gt}=0\text{ for }(g,t)\neq(2,4)\right)
\right|_{x=0}
=
-\frac{1}{10}.
\]
\item \textbf{(Local negative derivative.)} There exists \(\varepsilon>0\) such that, for every effect vector \(\delta'\), if \(|\delta'_{gt}|<\varepsilon\) for every \(g\in\mathcal C\) and \(t=1,\ldots,4\), and if \(\delta'\) has strictly positive treated effects in the sense of Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:strict-positive-effects}, then
\[
\left.
\frac{d}{dx}\,
\beta^\star\!\left(\delta' \text{ with } \delta'_{2,4}\text{ replaced by }x\right)
\right|_{x=\delta'_{2,4}}
<0 .
\]
\end{itemize}

7fadf6a67b835957695cdf0261fd0b5be7a1b190.title: Four-Cohort Sign Reversal
7fadf6a67b835957695cdf0261fd0b5be7a1b190.body:
Let \(T=4\), let \(\mathcal C=\{2,3,4,\infty\}\), set \(\pi_g=1/4\) for every \(g\in\mathcal C\), set \(\bar b_g=1\) for every \(g\in\mathcal C\), and set \(\gamma_{t0}=0\) for every \(t=1,\ldots,4\). Define
\[
\delta_{gt}=
\begin{cases}
\log 4, & (g,t)=(2,4),\\
\log(101/100), & D_{gt}=1 \text{ and } (g,t)\neq(2,4),\\
0, & D_{gt}=0 .
\end{cases}
\]
Then:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Region membership.)} The primitive \(W_4\) obtained from these shares, baselines, time effects, and proportional-effect log multipliers belongs to the sign-reversal region \(\mathcal R_4\) of Definition~\ref{obj:synth_23}.
\item \textbf{(Positive effects.)} For every \(g\in\mathcal C\) and \(t=1,\ldots,4\), if \(D_{gt}=1\), then \(\delta_{gt}>0\).
\item \textbf{(Largest treated effect.)} For every \(g\in\mathcal C\) and \(t=1,\ldots,4\), if \(D_{gt}=1\) and \((g,t)\neq(2,4)\), then \(\delta_{gt}<\delta_{2,4}\).
\item \textbf{(No-effect FWL residuals.)} Write \(\widetilde W_{gt}(0)\) for the weighted FWL residual of Definition~\ref{obj:def:weighted-fwl-residual} computed with all cohort-time effect coordinates set to zero. For every supported cell \((g,t)\),
\[
\widetilde W_{gt}(0)
=
\frac{1}{8}
\begin{pmatrix}
-3 & 3 & 1 & -1\\
-1 & -3 & 3 & 1\\
1 & -1 & -3 & 3\\
3 & 1 & -1 & -3
\end{pmatrix}_{g,t},
\]
where the rows are \(g=2,3,4,\infty\) and the columns are \(t=1,2,3,4\).
\item \textbf{(Late-cell residual.)} In particular,
\[
\widetilde W_{2,4}(0)=-\frac18 .
\]
\item \textbf{(Late-cell derivative at zero.)} Holding all other cohort-time effect coordinates equal to zero,
\[
\left.
\frac{d}{dx}\,
\beta^\star\!\left(\delta_{2,4}=x,\ \delta_{gt}=0\text{ for }(g,t)\neq(2,4)\right)
\right|_{x=0}
=
-\frac{1}{10}.
\]
\item \textbf{(Local negative derivative.)} There exists \(\varepsilon>0\) such that, for every effect vector \(\delta'\), if \(|\delta'_{gt}|<\varepsilon\) for every \(g\in\mathcal C\) and \(t=1,\ldots,4\), and if \(\delta'\) has strictly positive treated effects in the sense of Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:strict-positive-effects}, then
\[
\left.
\frac{d}{dx}\,
\beta^\star\!\left(\delta' \text{ with } \delta'_{2,4}\text{ replaced by }x\right)
\right|_{x=\delta'_{2,4}}
<0 .
\]
\end{itemize}

441d6831b564eb1be4d7902644c1b1fa1d925293.title: Four-Cohort Sign Reversal
441d6831b564eb1be4d7902644c1b1fa1d925293.body:
The four-cohort witness \(W_4\) is constructed with cohort support
\(\mathcal C=\{2,3,4,\infty\}\), equal shares \(\pi_g=1/4\), limiting baselines
\(\bar b_g=1\), untreated time component \(\gamma_{t0}=0\), and proportional-effect
log multipliers
\[
\delta_{gt}=
\begin{cases}
\log 4, & (g,t)=(2,4),\\
\log(101/100), & D_{gt}=1 \text{ and } (g,t)\neq(2,4),\\
0, & D_{gt}=0 .
\end{cases}
\]
Then:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Region membership.)} The primitive \(W_4\), restricted to the supported cohort-time cells, belongs to the sign-reversal region \(\mathcal R_T\) specialized to \(T=4\) in Definition~\ref{obj:def:global-sign-reversal-region}.
\item \textbf{(Positive effects.)} For every \(g\in\mathcal C\) and \(t\in\{1,2,3,4\}\), if \(D_{gt}=1\), then \(\delta_{gt}>0\).
\item \textbf{(Strict largest effect.)} For every \(g\in\mathcal C\) and \(t\in\{1,2,3,4\}\), if \(D_{gt}=1\) and \((g,t)\neq(2,4)\), then \(\delta_{gt}<\delta_{2,4}\).
\item \textbf{(Weighted FWL residuals.)} At the no-effect vector, the weighted FWL residual \(\widetilde W_{gt}\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:weighted-fwl-residual} equals
\[
\frac{1}{8}
\begin{pmatrix}
-3 & 3 & 1 & -1\\
-1 & -3 & 3 & 1\\
1 & -1 & -3 & 3\\
3 & 1 & -1 & -3
\end{pmatrix},
\]
with rows \(g=2,3,4,\infty\) and columns \(t=1,2,3,4\). In particular, \(\widetilde W_{2,4}=-1/8\).
\item \textbf{(Derivative at zero.)} Holding all other effects at zero,
\[
\left.\frac{d}{dx}\,
\beta^\star\!\left(\delta_{2,4}=x,\ \delta_{gt}=0\text{ for }(g,t)\neq(2,4)\right)
\right|_{x=0}
=-\frac{1}{10}.
\]
\item \textbf{(Local negative derivative.)} There exists \(\varepsilon>0\) such that, for every effect vector \(\delta'\), if
\[
|\delta'_{gt}|<\varepsilon
\quad\text{for all }g\in\mathcal C,\ t\in\{1,2,3,4\},
\]
and \(\delta'\) has strictly positive treated effects in the sense of Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:strict-positive-effects}, then
\[
\left.
\frac{d}{dx}\,
\beta^\star\!\left(\delta' \text{ with } \delta'_{2,4}\text{ replaced by }x\right)
\right|_{x=\delta'_{2,4}}
<0 .
\]
\end{itemize}
